rationale: Hard pass. The stick-figure "thief puzzle" fail trope relies on character-driven slapstick comedy, but Bounce Blast 3D is a character-free, calm/satisfying physics puzzle with a dark moody palette — there's no narrative framing or protagonist to anchor a fail gag. Mechanically there's zero overlap (pull-pin/thief logic vs. block-place + ball chain reactions), and while the audience age band loosely overlaps, the chaotic comedic tone attracts brain-test/hyper-casual seekers rather than the relaxed physics-clear crowd. Don't adapt this archetype; if leadership insists on a fail hook, rebuild it around a near-miss chain reaction instead.
